title: LRU Cache

examples.input:
capacity = 2
set(1, 10)
set(5, 12)
get(5) returns 12
get(1) returns 10
get(10) returns -1
set(6, 14)
get(5) returns -1

capacity = 3
set(1, 1)
set(2, 2)
set(3, 3)
get(1) returns 1
set(4, 4)
get(2) returns -1

hints: Use a doubly linked list to maintain the order of recently used items.
Use a hash map to store key -> node mappings for O(1) access.
The head of the list should point to the least recently used item.
The tail of the list should point to the most recently used item.
When accessing an item (get or set), move it to the tail (most recently used).
When the cache is full and you need to insert, remove the head node (LRU).
Use dummy head and tail nodes to simplify edge case handling.
